summary: Matched an initial 7.21% of melee/cm/camera.c by adding a new C translation unit with several camera functions, moving camera structs/prototypes into dedicated cm headers, and marking the corresponding scratch entries as OK. The PR also normalized local literal definitions by replacing the LITERAL macro with file-local static declarations in touched fighter/item/stage files and removed the global LITERAL macro from placeholder.h. Evidence is strong for file/function coverage and header reshaping, but there were no review comments explaining author intent.
what_changed: Added src/melee/cm/camera.c with 407 lines of camera decomp code, including Camera_80029020, Camera_80029BC4, Camera_8002A768, Camera_8002B1F8, Camera_80030DF8, Camera_80031154, and Camera_8003118C.

PR report records melee/cm/camera.c at 35.37 KB and 7.21% matched; Camera_8002B1F8, Camera_80029BC4, Camera_8003118C, Camera_80031154, Camera_80029020, and Camera_80030DF8 reached 100%, while Camera_8002A768 was 82.73%.

Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt to mark relevant camera scratch solutions as OK, including Camera_80028B9C, func_80029020, Camera_80029BC4, Camera_8002A768, Camera_8002B1F8, Camera_80030DF8, func_80031154, and func_8003118C.
Introduced src/melee/cm/forward.h with forward typedefs for Camera, CameraBounds, CameraBox, and CameraMovement.
Introduced src/melee/cm/types.h defining CameraBox, CameraMovement, CameraBounds, and Camera, then removed the older CameraBox typedef from ft/types.h and included cm/types.h there.

Reworked camera.h prototypes to use concrete camera types and address comments, replacing placeholder-heavy declarations such as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and struct _CameraBox with CameraBox*, bool, float, and Vec3* signatures where known.
Added camera.dox containing a Doxygen-style/generated view of camera declarations, including some still-unknown prototypes.
Updated player character typing from enum_t to CharacterKind in player.h/player.c and included ft/forward.h so camera code can compare against CHKIND_SANDBAG.
Removed an ad hoc extern declaration for Camera_80029044 from item.c now that camera.h provides the typed prototype.
Removed #define LITERAL static from placeholder.h and converted touched LITERAL declarations in itsword.c, ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c, and ground.c to explicit static declarations.
Reformatted asm/melee/cm/camera.s extensively with .file, .text/.balign, .fn/.endfn, .obj/.endobj, decimal rlwimi mask operands, explicit hex immediates, and typed float/double literals in sdata2.
smart_moves: Separated camera forward declarations and full camera struct definitions into cm/forward.h and cm/types.h, avoiding ownership of CameraBox inside fighter types and making camera APIs reusable by fighter, item, and player code.

Promoted camera.h prototypes from placeholder signatures to known typed signatures only where evidence from the new C code supported them, reducing local extern clutter such as the item.c Camera_80029044 declaration.
Kept a partial high-score function, Camera_8002A768 at 82.73%, in C alongside fully matched smaller functions, allowing incremental camera progress without blocking on one difficult routine.

Used existing vector/stage/player APIs in camera.c, such as lbVector_Diff, lbVector_Normalize, lbVector_Add, Stage_GetCamZoomRate, Stage_GetCamMaxDepth, Player_GetEntity, and Player_GetPlayerCharacter, rather than embedding raw behavior behind placeholders.
Normalized literals from the project-wide LITERAL macro to explicit static declarations, making storage duration visible at each declaration and allowing placeholder.h to shed a broad macro.
decomp_lessons: When a struct is shared across subsystems, move it to the owning system's types.h and expose only forward typedefs through forward.h; this PR moved CameraBox out of ft/types.h into cm/types.h.
Replacing per-file extern declarations with a typed owning header reduces duplicate signatures and helps subsequent matching work compile against consistent types.
Camera code depends on fighter, player, stage, vector math, HSD CObj, and trig/math headers; matching camera functions may require plumbing cross-system includes before function bodies can land.

A large asm churn can be mechanical rather than semantic: here camera.s mostly moved to modern function/object directives and literal formatting while C code and scratch status drove the decomp progress.
Typed character APIs matter for cross-system comparisons: changing Player_GetPlayerCharacter to CharacterKind supported code comparing the result to CHKIND_SANDBAG.
Do not overstate full decomp quality for partial functions: Camera_8002A768 remains 82.73% and the added source still contains M2C_ERROR markers, so it is useful but not final.
naming_conventions: Camera subsystem functions retain address-based names with the Camera_ prefix, e.g. Camera_80029BC4, Camera_8002A768, Camera_8002B1F8, Camera_80031154, and Camera_8003118C.
Camera types use subsystem-owned PascalCase typedefs: Camera, CameraBounds, CameraBox, and CameraMovement.

Struct fields in newly introduced camera types combine descriptive names where understood, such as target_interest, target_position, target_fov, x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max, z_pos, translation, nearz, and farz, with unk_/x-prefixed fields where meaning is unknown.
Header prototypes in camera.h are annotated with short address comments such as /* 029020 */, /* 030CD8 */, and /* 03118C */.
Known constants and globals retain address-based cm_ names, such as cm_80452C68, cm_803BCB64, cm_804D7E04, and cm_804D7E60.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marked completed scratch entries as OK in config/GALE01/scratches.txt after moving functions into C.
Converted camera.s from .global/function labels to .fn/.endfn blocks and from raw .section usage to .file "camera.c", .text, and .balign 4.
Converted sdata2 literal blobs from raw .4byte pairs to .obj/.endobj with explicit .float and .double values where known, such as 0.125, 6.283185307179586, and 3.141592653589793.
Used explicit hex immediates in asm formatting, e.g. 0x0, 0x4, and 0x8, and normalized some mnemonics such as addi-negative forms to subi/subic.
The diff shows sda21 operands rendered with r0 in the assembly output, suggesting toolchain/disassembly formatting changes rather than hand-authored C logic.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR context, so there is no recorded reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or improve Camera_8002A768 and remove M2C_ERROR markers.
Search for follow-up camera struct field renames replacing unk_2bc, unk_2c0, x10, x1C, x28, x2C, x34, x40, and x48.
Look for PRs that remove or reconcile camera.dox after camera.h gains more complete prototypes.
Search for additional removals of the old LITERAL macro pattern and conversions to explicit static declarations.
Find later camera PRs that type cm_80452C68, cm_803BCB64, and other cm_ globals more precisely.
